evidence.scientific_clarity: Force-directed spring layout with 4 community colors on 30 nodes matches brief exactly; edge alpha=0.25 and node_size=280 prevent hairball.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype=0 set in script; default DejaVu Sans used throughout — text will be Type 3 bitmapped in PDF.
evidence.palette: Categorical palette is appropriate for communities, but red (#E63946) + teal (#2A9D8F) pairing is problematic for red-green colorblind viewers.
evidence.layout_density: 7x7 inch figure with tight_layout(), axis off, and tuned k=0.55 spring layout makes good use of panel space with no obvious whitespace waste.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption accurately describes the figure and legend is present; white node labels at font_size=6 risk illegibility at journal print size.
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf exists (18 KB, non-trivial) but script omits pdf.fonttype=42, so text is embedded as Type 3 bitmapped fonts rather than selectable outlines.
evidence.file_format: All required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (18 KB), figure.png (121 KB), caption.tex (355 B), gen_figure.py (1.7 KB); bonus figure.svg also produced.
evidence.caption_quality: Names algorithm (Fruchterman-Reingold), mentions fixed seed for reproducibility, and makes a qualitative claim about intra-community edge dominance, but provides no hard numerical counts.

overall_strengths: Layout and visual design are well-executed: tuned spring layout, reduced edge alpha, white node outlines, and tight_layout produce a clean, readable network figure.
All required output files are present and non-trivial, and the caption accurately describes the figure with algorithm-level specificity.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype=0) means text in the PDF/SVG is bitmapped Type 3, not selectable vector — a common journal rejection trigger.
Red-teal color pairing in the community palette is not safe for red-green colorblind readers; swapping one for a blue-family or purple hue would fix this.
